title: Pinalia retroflexa
taxonomy: Pinalia retroflexa is classified in the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family is one of the largest and most diverse plant families, with over 30,000 known species. Within the Orchidaceae family, Pinalia retroflexa belongs to the subtribe Aeridinae, which includes species of orchids that are often epiphytic or terrestrial. The genus Pinalia is a small genus within the subtribe, containing approximately 25 species. Pinalia retroflexa is native to tropical Asia, specifically in the regions of India and Southeast Asia.
uses: Pinalia retroflexa is reported to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. It is believed to have antimalarial properties and is used in the treatment of fever and other related symptoms. The plant is also used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Additionally, Pinalia retroflexa is used in traditional medicine to treat digestive problems and improve overall health. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of these medicinal uses.
